items.title: Family still reeling after death of baby in regional WA

children.content: The parents of Willow Horne are still looking for answers after the eight-month-old died in a regional WA hospital.

items.title: Woman attacked by dog pack in WA's far north

image.altText: Street sign with long road with car approaching bend in background

children.content: The woman says 10 dogs were being walked off-leash in Kununurra when seven attacked her and her dog. She says the owners did nothing to assist.

items.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport

children.content: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

image.altText: Children look at a laptop. The word Glencore can be seen on the lid. A person in an orange high-vis shirt is nearby.

items.title: Disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service resumes after years of delays

children.content: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

image.altText: Exterior of the new Australind train running between Perth and Bunbury.

items.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row

children.content: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant 51-point victory over Gold Coast at Perth Stadium — a result that puts them two games clear at the top of the AFL ladder.

gallery.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

thumbnail.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

items.title: Key observatory shut for months prior to bird flu detection

children.content: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

A timber sign reads "Welcome to Eyre Bird Observatory, Wonundra".

items.title: Two things this week gave clues about the global future

children.content: Two things happened last week that give us a much clearer picture of what the world is going to be like from now on.

image.altText: Chinese President Xi Jinping attends the closing session of the National People's Congress.

items.title: Thai teenager's father 'deeply saddened' as Australian charged with murder

children.content: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

image.altText: A teary man with a black tshirt slung over his shoulder, speaks into a small microphone as a woman in yellow sits beside him

items.title: Quick hits: Canada's emotional match-winner and South Korea's team blasted

children.content: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here are the five quick hits from the World Cup.

articles.title: Canada 'heroes' score stunning injury time winner to down South Africa

children.content: World Cup co-host Canada beats South Africa to progress into the last 16 thanks to a last-gasp strike from Stephen Eustaquio in Los Angeles.

image.altText: Stephen Eustaquio celebrates

articles.title: Socceroos to play Brazil at Lang Park after World Cup

children.content: The first of the two friendlies will be at Brisbane's Lang Park on September 25, and the second at Townsville on September 29.

image.altText: Australian fan with green and gold sunglasses and a funny hat

Myung-Bo Hong looks to one side

items.title: Taylor brushes off low polling and says rebuilding trust takes time

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says it will take time for the Coalition to win back the trust of voters. Follow live.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: WA shearing teams look for new ways to make ends meet

children.content: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.

image.altText: a lady sitting in a tractor

items.title: Real estate has been an easy target for criminals laundering money, until now

children.content: Anti-money laundering laws mean that for the first time, real estate agents, lawyers, accountants and others must report suspicious transactions.

articles.title: Major capital cities record worst housing auction clearance rates in years

children.content: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

image.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

articles.title: Treasurer promises to 'fix' property tax oversight, but won't say how exactly

children.content: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial shot of dozens of houses in neat rows in a new outer-suburban housing estate.

items.title: Age, scandals and pressure push Ben Stokes into retirement

children.content: England captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the third Test against New Zealand, the team has said.

image.altText: England captain Ben Stokes standing in the field

items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

image.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting

items.title: How Ben overcame his dilemma about disclosing his autism in job interviews

children.content: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

gallery.altText: A man types on a computer.

thumbnail.altText: A man types on a computer.

items.title: History quiz: Test your knowledge from this decade to the 1600s

children.content: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

image.altText: Eminem performs onstage at the 53rd annual Grammy Awards

items.title: Race Around The World recap: Catch this week's films

children.content: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.

items.title: 1,000kg of CSIRO archives on Australia's wool history returned

children.content: More than 1,000 kilograms of wool samples, lab notes and technical drawings spent decades gathering dust in a British home. A Victorian sheep farmer just brought them back.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a sheep lying on a table surrounded by scientific equipment on a farm, taken by Dr Harold Carter

items.title: Piastri has 'really good day', Russell survives late charge in Austria

children.content: Oscar Piastri has hailed his fourth place at Sunday's Austrian Grand Prix as a "really good day", while Mercedes George Russell held on to claim his first victory since the start of the season.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri holding a microphone and smiling during a media conference

items.title: Why does Perth have an obsession with double brick homes unlike any other city?

children.content: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

A construction worker is working at the site of building a house.
A house is being built at a construction site.

thumbnail.altText: A couple smile to the camera at a house construction site.
A construction worker is working at the site of building a house.
A house is being built at a construction site.

items.title: Perry and Gardner dominate India to get World Cup revenge

children.content: A powerful partnership by Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carries Australia to the greatest run chase in Women's T20 World Cup history, knocking India out of the tournament.

image.altText: Ellyse Perry hugs Ash Gardner during a T20 World Cup match.

items.title: Telstra phasing out CAN Radio phone system amid customer safety concerns

children.content: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

image.altText: Telstra worker about to enter a manhole.

items.title: More rain on the way for WA after storm damages roofs, downs trees

children.content: Perth residents are bracing for more wet weather after a powerful cold front damaged roofs and downed trees on Friday night.

image.altText: A tree lies across the roof of a house with SES crews in attendance

items.title: Couple welcomes refugees to home 'like family' in resettlement program

children.content: Margaret River residents Anne and John Roberts's motto is to offer a "soft landing" and a "warm embrace" to refugees as they settle into life in Australia.

image.altText: A woman in a blue floral top standing next tot a man in a brown 3/4 zip fleece holding cups of tea on a verandah

items.title: Photographer captures elders' stories and WA's stunning southern landscapes

children.content: A photography exhibition in Albany shares the stories of Menang elders, who often have been overlooked in history.
